description: Create a hero section with headline 'Tastinski Pro Painters: Seattle's Premier AI-Powered Painting Service' and subheadline 'Experience the future of painting. Precision, quality, and efficiency for your home or business. Serving Kent, Bellevue, Redmond, and beyond.' Include a 'Get Your AI-Fast Quote Now' CTA button. Use a professional background image showing a beautifully painted Seattle home exterior or modern interior. Color scheme: navy blue, white, gray with gold or orange accents.

Add a Services section titled 'Our Comprehensive Painting Solutions' with three service cards: Interior Painting (walls, ceilings, trim, cabinets), Exterior Painting (siding, decks, fences), and Commercial Painting (offices, retail spaces). Each card should have an icon, title, and brief description. Use the navy and gold color scheme.

Add a 'The Tastinski Difference: Smarter Painting' section explaining how AI technology provides faster, more accurate estimates and streamlined project management. Highlight that this means less hassle for customers and perfect finishes every time. Use professional, customer-focused language.

Add a Portfolio/Gallery section titled 'Our Work Speaks for Itself' showing a grid of before-and-after photos of painting projects. Include categories for interior and exterior work to showcase quality and versatility.

Add a Trust Signals section titled 'Licensed, Bonded, and Insured' displaying credentials confirming Washington State contractor registration (L&I compliance), surety bond, and liability insurance. Use professional badges or text to build trust with potential clients.

Add a Testimonials section titled 'What Our Clients Say' featuring 3-4 authentic customer reviews with names, project types, and star ratings. Make them compelling and highlight quality, professionalism, and customer satisfaction.

Add an About Us section titled 'Meet the Team' with a brief story about the company, its mission to revolutionize the painting industry with technology and craftsmanship, and founder information (Aidyn/AK if applicable). Keep it authentic and mission-driven.

Add a Contact/Quote section (can be in footer) titled 'Ready to Transform Your Space?' with a simple contact form requesting name, email, phone, project type, and brief project description. Include phone number, email address, and mention of service areas (Kent, Bellevue, Redmond, Kirkland, Greater Seattle).

Transform the entire website to a Quiet Luxury aesthetic targeting high-net-worth homeowners. Update the color palette: Primary Deep Slate Blue or Charcoal for headers, Secondary Sage Green or Muted Olive accents, Background Warm Off-white or Champagne (not pure white), and Muted Gold or Deep Terracotta for CTAs and accents. Replace all typography with Playfair Display serif for titles and headings, and a clean refined sans-serif like Montserrat or similar for body text. Add generous high-quality whitespace throughout to give content room to breathe. Implement subtle gradients and soft shadows for depth. Apply warm filters to all images to create a premium home transformation vibe. The overall aesthetic should resemble a high-end architectural portfolio with sophisticated, muted tones - not a generic contractor page.

Completely rebuild the website with a master craftsmanship aesthetic. HERO SECTION: Use high-resolution architectural photograph of a luxury modern Pacific Northwest home nestled in lush forest (cedar, fir trees) with golden hour or overcast lighting. Headline: 'Tastinski: The Art of Master Painting'. Subheadline: 'Premium residential and commercial painting for Seattle's most distinguished homes. Dedicated to perfection in every brushstroke across Bellevue, Redmond, and beyond.' Two CTA buttons: Primary 'Request Private Consultation' (Sage Green #8B9D83 background, white text) and Secondary 'View Our Work' (outlined, transparent). COLOR PALETTE: Primary Sage Green #8B9D83, Secondary Charcoal #2C2C2C, Accent Warm Off-white #FAF9F6. TYPOGRAPHY: Elegant serif for headers, clean sans-serif for body. Remove ALL mentions of AI, technology, automation. Focus on master craftsmanship, quiet luxury, Ritz-Carlton level service.

Add THE TASTINSKI STANDARDS section with headline 'The Difference is in the Details'. Three columns: Column 1 - Meticulous Preparation: 'Every project begins with thorough surface preparation. We don't cut corners—we power wash, scrape, sand, prime, and caulk. The foundation of lasting beauty.' Column 2 - Premium Materials: 'We exclusively use Sherwin-Williams Emerald and Benjamin Moore Aura—paints engineered for the Pacific Northwest climate. 12-15 year lifespan, not 3-5.' Column 3 - White-Glove Service: 'Spotless job sites. Daily cleanup. Furniture protection. Transparent communication. We treat your home with the respect it deserves.'

Add SERVICES section with headline 'Our Services'. Four service cards in grid: Card 1 - Exterior Painting: 'Transform your home's presence with weather-resistant finishes built for Seattle's climate.' Price Range: $6,000 - $12,000. Card 2 - Interior Painting: 'Refined color palettes and flawless application for living spaces that inspire.' Price Range: $3,500 - $7,000. Card 3 - Architectural Trim: 'Precision work on crown molding, baseboards, and cabinetry—where craftsmanship shows.' Price Range: Custom Quote. Card 4 - Commercial Projects: 'Boutique offices, restaurants, and retail spaces painted with minimal disruption.' Price Range: Custom Quote. CTA: 'Schedule an Estimate'

Add GALLERY section with headline 'Recent Work'. Grid of 6-9 high-quality placeholder images showing: luxury home exteriors (modern style), Victorian home detail (trim work), interior living rooms (accent walls), commercial storefronts (clean lines), before/after comparisons, architectural details (window trim, precision work). Use luxury aesthetic placeholders only.

Add PROCESS section with headline 'How We Work'. Timeline layout with 5 steps: Step 1 - Private Consultation: 'We visit your property, discuss your vision, assess the scope, and provide a detailed written estimate.' Step 2 - Material Selection: 'Together, we select premium paints and finishes. We explain options and recommend what works best for your project.' Step 3 - Preparation & Protection: 'Meticulous surface prep. Furniture covered, floors protected, landscaping safeguarded.' Step 4 - Expert Application: 'Our master craftsmen apply 2-3 coats with precision. Daily progress updates.' Step 5 - Final Walkthrough: 'We inspect together. Touch-ups completed immediately. You approve before final payment.' Include relevant icons for each step.

Add SERVICE AREAS section with headline 'Serving Seattle's Finest Neighborhoods'. Elegant list: Ballard, Queen Anne, Capitol Hill, Fremont, Wallingford, Bellevue, Redmond, Kirkland, Medina. Subtext: 'Licensed, insured, and trusted by Seattle homeowners since [year]'

Add CONTACT section with headline 'Begin Your Transformation'. Two-column layout: Left - Contact form with fields: Full Name (required), Phone Number (required), Email (required), Project Type dropdown (Exterior Painting / Interior Painting / Commercial / Other), Property Address, Message (optional multiline). Button: 'Request Consultation' (Sage Green). Right - Contact info: Phone (206) XXX-XXXX (large, clickable tel: link), Email contact@tastinski.com (clickable mailto:), Business Hours: Monday-Saturday 8 AM - 6 PM, Address: Serving Greater Seattle Area.

Add refined FOOTER. Left side: Tastinski Pro Painters, 'Master Craftsmanship Since [Year]'. Center: Links for Services, Service Areas, Contact. Right side: Licensed & Insured, Contractor License #XXXXXX. Bottom bar: '© 2025 Tastinski Pro Painters. All rights reserved.' Clean, minimal design matching quiet luxury aesthetic.

Update hero section background to feature a stately Victorian home (Queen Anne style) in the Pacific Northwest. Architecture should show classic Victorian with ornate trim, turret, wraparound porch. Setting: nestled in lush forest with towering cedar and fir trees. Paint colors: freshly painted in sage green body, cream trim, deep charcoal accents. Lighting: soft golden hour or overcast. Style: historic charm meets impeccable maintenance. Keep existing headline 'Tastinski: The Art of Master Painting' and subheadline. Maintain Sage Green CTAs. Overall aesthetic: Victorian elegance, not modern minimalism.

Add new FREE INSTANT ESTIMATE section with headline 'Get Your Instant Estimate' and subheadline 'Answer 4 quick questions for a preliminary quote range'. Interactive calculator with 4 questions: Q1 Project Type (radio buttons: Exterior/Interior/Both/Commercial), Q2 Property Size (1-2 Bedrooms/3-4 Bedrooms/5+ Bedrooms/Multi-unit), Q3 Current Condition (Excellent/Good/Fair), Q4 Timeframe (Within 1 month/1-3 months/3-6 months/Just exploring). Calculate button 'Get Estimate Range' in Sage Green. Result displays 'Your Estimated Range: $X,XXX - $X,XXX' with note 'This is a preliminary estimate. For an exact quote, schedule a free consultation.' Then CTA button 'Schedule Free On-Site Consultation'. Use pricing logic: Exterior 2-bed $4k-6k, 3-4 bed $6k-9k, 5+ bed $9k-15k; Interior 2-bed $2.5k-4k, 3-4 bed $3.5k-6k, 5+ bed $5k-8k; Both: add together; Commercial: 'Custom quote required'; Fair condition: +20%. Make fully functional and interactive.

Update gallery section to feature Victorian and historic architecture styles. Change image placeholders to show: Victorian home exterior (freshly painted, ornate trim, vibrant colors), Craftsman home (before/after comparison), Interior living room (accent wall), Historic home detail (window trim, architectural elements), Commercial storefront (clean lines), Architectural detail (crown molding, baseboard, precision work). Prefer Victorian and historic architecture styles over modern. Maintain luxury aesthetic and grid layout.

Update footer left side to show 'Tastinski Pro Painters' and 'Master Craftsmanship' (remove 'Since [Year]'). Keep center navigation links (Services, Service Areas, Contact) and right side credentials (Licensed & Insured, Contractor License #XXXXXX). Maintain copyright bar. Ensure refined Victorian elegance aesthetic throughout.

Update the hero section background image to show a stately Victorian home (Queen Anne style with ornate trim, turret, wraparound porch) painted in sage green body with cream trim and charcoal accents. CRITICAL REQUIREMENT: The background setting must be a DENSE CONIFEROUS EVERGREEN FOREST with ONLY evergreen trees that have NEEDLES (Douglas fir, Western red cedar, Sitka spruce, hemlock). Dark green needle foliage. Think Christmas trees, pine trees, fir trees. Misty Pacific Northwest rainforest atmosphere. Trees must be clearly CONIFEROUS/EVERGREEN with visible pine/fir needles. Dense forest setting with moody, dramatic natural Pacific Northwest environment. Absolutely NO oak, maple, birch, or any deciduous trees with leaves. NO tropical plants or palms. ONLY evergreen coniferous needle trees. Lighting: soft golden hour or overcast. Add subtle dark overlay for text readability. The image must emphasize the Pacific Northwest rainforest aesthetic with dense evergreen conifers surrounding the Victorian home.

Replace the hero section background image with a GRAND THREE-STORY VICTORIAN MANSION as the main prominent subject. MAIN SUBJECT: Stately Victorian mansion (NOT cabin, NOT cottage). Architecture must show: Three-story Queen Anne Victorian style, ornate decorative gingerbread trim work, round corner turret with conical roof, large wraparound porch with turned posts, bay windows with decorative frames, multiple gables and rooflines, freshly painted in sage green siding with cream trim and charcoal accents, well-maintained luxury residential property. COMPOSITION: House takes up 60-70% of frame as main focus, clearly visible architectural details, luxury home prominence. BACKGROUND SETTING: Dense evergreen coniferous forest (Douglas fir, cedar with dark green needles) BEHIND the house providing backdrop atmosphere, trees frame house but don't hide it, forest visible behind and around house (20-30% of frame), Pacific Northwest setting. This is a GRAND LUXURY VICTORIAN MANSION with ornate painted details, NOT a rustic cabin. The forest is the BACKGROUND, the mansion is the STAR. Think expensive historic home in Seattle's Queen Anne neighborhood with trees behind it. Lighting: golden hour or soft overcast. Maintain 20-30% dark overlay for text readability.

Replace hero background image with professional real estate photography style showing a LARGE VICTORIAN HOME (Queen Anne style) as the main subject. COMPOSITION: House positioned in center and clearly visible (60% of frame), front yard with landscaping in foreground (10%), tall CONIFEROUS EVERGREEN trees (Douglas fir, cedar with pine/fir needles - NOT deciduous leaves) surrounding and framing the house from behind and sides (30%), dappled natural lighting through tree canopy, lush Pacific Northwest atmosphere. HOUSE DETAILS: Freshly painted sage green siding, cream trim, charcoal accents, ornate trim details, turret, wraparound porch clearly visible, well-maintained luxury property. LIGHTING: Natural daylight, soft even lighting, trees create natural shade and depth, professional real estate photography quality, warm inviting luxury home aesthetic. This is a GRAND Victorian mansion (NOT cabin), professional real estate listing standard quality for $1-2M Seattle Victorian home surrounded by Pacific Northwest evergreen forest. Trees MUST be coniferous/evergreen with needles, not deciduous trees with leaves.

Update the hero section background image to use the locally generated Victorian mansion image at ./victorian-hero.png. This image shows a grand three-story Victorian Queen Anne mansion with ornate trim, turret with conical roof, wraparound porch, bay windows, multiple gables, painted in sage green with cream trim. The house is surrounded by tall Pacific Northwest coniferous evergreen trees (Douglas fir, cedar with dark green needles) that frame the property. Professional real estate photography composition with house centered (60% of frame), trees surrounding from behind and sides (30%), and manicured lawn with landscaping in foreground (10%). Natural soft daylight, luxury $1-2M historic property aesthetic. Remove the old Unsplash image URL and use the local image path instead. Adjust overlay opacity to 20-30% to maintain text readability while showing the beautiful Victorian architecture and evergreen forest setting.

Reposition the hero section text content to the left side of the image, overlaying the forest area (not the house). Place all text (headline, subheadline, and CTA buttons) inside a semi-transparent dark box with background color rgba(44, 44, 44, 0.75), padding of 40px, and border-radius of 8px. This will keep the beautiful Victorian mansion clearly visible in the center while making the text perfectly readable against the darker forest background on the left. Adjust the layout so the text container is left-aligned and the house remains the prominent visual focus in the center-right of the frame.

Zoom out the hero background image by 15-20%. The Victorian mansion should remain prominent but show more context - more surrounding evergreen forest visible on the sides, more foreground lawn and garden landscaping visible. Create a more spacious, grand estate feeling with the house still as the focal point but with expanded view of the property and Pacific Northwest setting.

Add a new optional square footage input field to the FREE INSTANT ESTIMATE calculator. Insert it as Question 2 (between Project Type and Property Size). Add a number input field with placeholder text 'Enter approximate square feet' and helper text below saying 'Don't know? Select property size in next question'. This question is OPTIONAL and can be skipped. Update question numbering: Q1 Project Type, Q2 Square Footage (NEW - optional), Q3 Property Size (now optional if sqft provided), Q4 Current Condition, Q5 Timeframe.

Update the calculator pricing logic to use square footage when provided. NEW FORMULA: If square footage is entered, calculate: Exterior painting at $2.50-$4.00 per sqft (based on condition - Excellent $2.50-$3.00, Good $3.00-$3.50, Fair $3.50-$4.00), Interior painting at $1.50-$3.00 per sqft (Excellent $1.50-$2.00, Good $2.00-$2.50, Fair $2.50-$3.00). Show the calculation formula in results like 'Based on approximately 2,000 sqft at $2.50-$3.50 per sqft'. If square footage is NOT provided, use the existing bedroom-based ranges. Make Property Size optional when sqft is provided.

Make the 'Request Private Consultation' button in the hero section scroll smoothly down to the contact form section at the bottom of the page when clicked. Add smooth scroll behavior so users are taken directly to the 'Begin Your Transformation' contact form section.

Add email notification functionality to the contact form. When someone submits the form with their details (Full Name, Phone Number, Email, Project Type, Property Address, Message), send an email notification to kapodze@gmail.com containing all the submitted information. Include proper email formatting with all field labels and values. Ensure the form submission triggers the email successfully.

Update the contact phone number throughout the website from (253) 315-1253 to (360) 504-8767. Change it in the contact section, form placeholders, and anywhere else it appears. Make sure the tel: link is tel:+13605048767
